subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequence and logical development with wellâlabelled sections (introduction â models â evaluation â conclusion)
Effective use of headings, subâheadings and numbered stages to guide the reader
Appendix used appropriately to keep main text concise
weaknesses: Table of contents contains incorrect page ranges, hindering navigation
Lowâresolution graphs make data points hard to read
Occasional redundancy and mislabelled residual tables reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with clear organization (band 3). Minor lapses in the table of contents, figure labelling and some redundant narrative prevent it from reaching the exemplary band 4.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, GeoGebra graphs, SSR/RSE comparisons)
Generally consistent use of standard mathematical vocabulary (gradient, residual, SSR)
Logical linking of figures and tables to the narrative
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (mix of commas/periods in numbers, plain-text âlnâ)
Inconsistent rounding of parameters and occasional grammatical awkwardness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Multiple representations are used effectively and communication is largely clear (band 3). While notation and formatting inconsistencies exist, the overall clarity and structure justify a score of 3 and support the total mark requirement.

strengths: Personal connection in the introduction demonstrates genuine motivation
Manual derivation of parameters and extension to RSE show independent thinking
Defence of model choices using self-devised criteria adds personal voice
weaknesses: Exploration follows a conventional modelling template
Limited novelty in mathematical manoeuvres beyond standard methods
Creative initiative is present but not sustained throughout
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach (band 2), but creativity and innovation remain at a solid rather than outstanding level.

strengths: Identification of limitations (ignoring inflation, small data set) reflects awareness
Some qualitative discussion of over- and underâestimation indicates engagement with results
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive with minimal depth
Lacks quantitative evaluation of outcomes or sensitivity analysis
No discussion of future investigations or methodological implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but limited and lacks depth or critical insight (band 1). The qualitative comments do not extend to substantial evaluation or future implications.

strengths: All mathematics is directly relevant to the research question and model comparison
Techniques (linear, quadratic, logarithmic, SSR, RSE) are commensurate with SL level
Extension to RSE demonstrates sound understanding and appropriate rigor
weaknesses: Minor methodological flaws (using only two points for slope, substitution errors)
Inconsistent rounding and lack of unit labelling in SSR values
Opportunities for full leastâsquares regression and clarity on parameter count not taken
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is fully commensurate with course level and directly addresses the aim (band 4). Minor errors and methodological lapses prevent a higher mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments normally proceed step-by-step, but algebraic workings are occasionally compressed (e.g. simultaneous-equation solution shown as block text) and some explanatory sentences are grammatically awkward, detracting from clarity.  Hence the communication is adequate rather than good.

Linear parameters are found correctly; quadratic coefficients contain rounding but are otherwise sensible; logarithmic verification is accurate.  Some methodological flaws (using only two points for slope, inconsistent rounding, squaring residuals without unit discussion) reveal minor misunderstandings, so understanding is good with some lapses rather than thorough and completely correct.

Raw-data tables, three GeoGebra graphs, residual tables, and a comparative SSR/RSE table present the same information in several forms and are generally well linked to the text.  This constitutes effective multiple representations, even though figure resolution is sometimes low.

The investigation follows a clear sequence (introduction â data â three models â evaluation â conclusion).  Headings and transitional commentary guide the reader so that the mathematical story is easy to follow, even when the student moves back and forth between tables and graphs.  Minor lapsesâduplicate or misleading page references in the contents list and occasional digressions inside the residual-discussion paragraphsâprevent the work from reaching the exemplary standard.

Apart from introducing RSE, there is limited evidence of creative or original mathematical manoeuvres; the modelling techniques and comparisons employed are standard fare.  Therefore, only some initiative is demonstrated.

Critical analysis is present in the form of noting over-/under-estimation and economic events, but these comments remain qualitative and are not pursued to examine parameter sensitivity or alternative models.  The analysis, while existing, is basic.

The student recognises limitations (e.g., ignoring inflation, small data set) and mentions that greater granularity could help, but no concrete strategy or future investigation is proposed.  Consequently, evaluation of outcomes remains limited.

The reflection section mainly lists skills gained and briefly mentions Romaniaâs dependence on EU economics.  There is little depth or probing consideration of what the results imply for the model or for Romania beyond surface comments, so reflection is limited.

Standard mathematical vocabulary (gradient, residual, SSR, RSE) and symbolic notation are used, yet inconsistently: commas and full-stops mix in large numbers, âlnâ is typeset in plain text, and the gradient is quoted to two decimal places but then reused to five-figure accuracy.  These inconsistencies lower the quality from âmostly consistentâ to âsome appropriate useâ.

The rationale connects the topic to the studentâs personal background, and model selection is defended using personally-devised criteria (SSR and RSE).  These elements provide a clear personal voice, yet the exploration still largely follows a conventional modelling template, so the engagement is solid but not outstanding.

Most material is relevant to modelling salary data, but lengthy narrative on Romanian economics and repeated explanations of residuals add noticeable redundancy.  Occasional repetition of the same residual table inside the appendix reduces conciseness, so the strand sits at a satisfactory rather than strong level.

All mathematics (linear, quadratic, logarithmic models, residuals, SSR, RSE) is directly relevant to the aim of finding the best model.  Every technique contributes to addressing the research question, showing effective use.

Choosing to create all parameters manually instead of relying on software, introducing RSE (outside the syllabus) and linking residual patterns to EU accession show noticeable independent thinking.  The ideas are not novel in a modelling IA, but they go beyond routine textbook procedures.

A logical three-part structure (set-up, modelling, judgement) is in place and reinforced with sub-headings and numbered stages inside each model.  The appendix houses supporting tables, keeping the main body readable.  However, the table of contents contains several incorrect page ranges and the labelling of some figures is inconsistent, so the overall organisation is competent rather than exemplary.

The algebra, residual computations and error measures are comfortably within, and fully commensurate with, SL level.  Extension to RSE is a slight elevation but still accessible, demonstrating sound appropriateness.

annotations.comment: The function definition uses plain text âlnâ rather than TeXâstyled math. Use $$\ln(x)$$ in display or inline math to maintain consistency in notation.
When introducing variables, explicitly state $$f(x)$$ as net income predicted and $$x$$ as years since 2000 in the same equation block for clarity.
The gradient m is rounded to 757.62 but utilized later at higher precision, creating inconsistency. Keep a single significantâfigure practice or quote m to at least the same accuracy throughout.
Choosing only two points (x=0 and x=8) for gradient calculation omits data variation beyond 2008. Consider using least squares formulas over all points to reduce selection bias.

The page of contents shows several incorrect page ranges (e.g., âData Collection and Analysisâ listed as 4â2). This misleads the reader and affects navigability. Update and verify each page reference in the table of contents.
The studentâs personal connection in the introduction is compelling, grounding the exploration in genuine motivation and showing clear independent thinking.
The final sentence in the aims paragraph ends abruptly (âand thereafterâ). Ensure each approach description is completed to clarify planned steps and maintain logical flow.
In the raw data table, commas and full stops are mixed in large numbers (e.g. â1,526.35â). Adopt a consistent numerical format (e.g., 1526.35) to improve clarity.

The notation â(RÂ²)â is used to denote sum of squared residuals, but RÂ² conventionally indicates coefficient of determination. Rename SSR consistently as SSR in math notation: $$SSR = \sum r_i^2$$.
The graph image resolution is low, making data points and axes labels hard to read. Replace with a higher-resolution export or recreate in Desmos for sharper output.
The residuals table repeats years 2009 and 2010 in the wrong order, causing confusion. Check row alignment to ensure each year matches the correct residual.
The sum of squared residuals table uses inconsistent decimal places (two vs four digits in the quadratic column). Standardise to a uniform format such as two decimal places.

The discussion of residuals notes economic events qualitatively, but does not quantify how sensitive parameters are to these events. Include a brief sensitivity analysis to deepen critical evaluation.
In solving for b, the algebra uses 7597.31 instead of 7587.31 (actual y-value). Verify the substitution step to ensure the correct intercept is obtained.
In the logarithmic model section, âlnâ appears outside math mode. Render as $$\ln(x)$$ within LaTeX to ensure consistent mathematical notation.
The sum of squared residuals table lacks units. Label each column with â(â¬Â²)â or note that SSR values are in squared euros for precision.
The RSE formula introduces p as the number of parameters but does not clarify its application per model. Clarify pâ=â2 or 3 when calculating each RSE for transparency.
The euro symbol is substituted by â\varepsilonâ in residual descriptions (e.g., â-\varepsilon10â). Use â¬ in text and math mode as $$-10.00\,â¬$$ to avoid confusion.
Selecting only three points for the quadratic model (endpoints + mid) risks overfitting those regions. Consider least squares regression over all data for a more representative fit.
The simultaneous-equation steps are embedded in a code block, reducing readability. Format algebra in LaTeX equations step by step for clearer mathematical communication.
The final quadratic equation is quoted as $$f(x)=4.84x^2+480.1x+1526.35$$ but intermediate values were approximated differently. Review rounding consistency across all stages.
The quadratic-model evaluation largely describes errors but omits direct comparison of SSR or RÂ² to justify its superiority. Include quantitative metrics in this section.
The reflection section briefly lists skills gained but lacks probing insights into what the results imply for the modelling process. Develop deeper critical reflection on methodology choices.
The World Bank citation in the prediction section lacks a proper date and URL in the reference list. Provide a complete citation to strengthen credibility.
The conclusion reaffirms steps taken rather than reflecting on limitations or implications. Expand on potential future work or alternative models for a more comprehensive closure.
